research.interface: GitLab REST API v4 projects endpoint with keyset pagination

official_sources.title: REST API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: base path /api/v4; PRIVATE-TOKEN header auth; keyset pagination via pagination=keyset with order_by/sort/per_page, next page delivered in the Link header (rel="next", carrying id_after=... for id ordering) with the end of collection marked by an absent Link header and empty array; offset pagination headers x-next-page, x-page, x-per-page, x-total, x-total-pages, x-prev-page; moved endpoints respond with a message and the new endpoint in the Location header ('moved permanently', GA in GitLab 16.7); null boolean fields are neither true nor false; NAMESPACE/PROJECT_PATH must be URL-encoded (%2F)

official_sources.title: Projects API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /projects attributes including order_by (id supported for keyset), sort asc/desc, id_after filter, membership/owned/simple; guidance to use keyset-based pagination to list more than 50,000 projects; project response fields id, path_with_namespace, visibility (private|internal|public), archived, and nullable booleans such as emails_disabled

research.language_rationale: The asset-inventory tooling this feeds is Ruby, and GitLab documents project listing as plain REST over /api/v4 with header auth, Link-header keyset pagination, and offset headers; Ruby's stdlib net/http and json exercise that documented surface hermetically with zero gems, so raw REST in Ruby is the credible integration path.

verified_facts: The GitLab REST API lives under /api/v4 and authenticates personal access tokens with the PRIVATE-TOKEN request header.
Keyset pagination is requested with pagination=keyset plus order_by/sort/per_page; for the projects endpoint ordered by id the next-page Link URL carries id_after=<last seen id>.
The next keyset page must be requested through the URL in the Link header's rel="next" entry; when the collection is exhausted the Link header is absent and the array is empty.
Offset pagination uses page/per_page and reports x-next-page (empty on the last page) among x-page, x-per-page, x-total, x-total-pages, x-prev-page.
GitLab recommends keyset-based pagination to list more than 50,000 projects.
After a path change the API responds that the resource 'has been moved permanently' and the new endpoint is given in the Location header; clients must use that endpoint.
Some boolean fields in API responses can be null; a null boolean has no default value and is neither true nor false, so consumers must not collapse it to false.
Namespaced project paths in URLs are percent-encoded, / as %2F.
